# SPEC: #333 â€” [FEAT] No home for reference algorithm spec â€” new optional play needed between prepare and implement
<!-- Generated by start-feature-planning | Plan sub-agent output -->

**Intent:** Add an optional play `algorithm` that sits between `prepare` and `implement` in the Garura pipeline. When an issue's tech.yaml contains algorithmically complex internal interfaces (identified by complexity indicators in `internal_interfaces[].behavior_contracts` or an explicit `algorithm_spec_required: true` flag), this play dispatches `tech-architect` with a new skill `draft-reference-algorithms` to produce `reference-algorithms.md` â€” a language-agnostic pseudocode specification for those interfaces. The artifact is placed into the existing STM context package at `{stm_base}/{issue}/context/design/reference-algorithms.md` as a sibling to tech.yaml. CRUD epics with no complexity indicators exit gracefully with a skip notice.

**Constraints:**
- C1: Play directory is `core/components/plays/algorithm/`; SKILL.md compiled from `reference/intent.yaml` via `/create-play --build algorithm`
- C2: Play is OPTIONAL â€” pre-flight detects algorithmic complexity indicators in tech.yaml; graceful skip (exit code 0) if none found
- C3: Complexity detection scans `internal_interfaces[].behavior_contracts` fields for indicator patterns: state machine, scheduler, retry, backoff, reconciliation, consensus, leader election, conflict resolution, merkle, diff/patch, compaction
- C4: An explicit `algorithm_spec_required: true` flag on any `internal_interfaces` entry triggers the play unconditionally regardless of C3 scan results
- C5: tech.yaml is READ-ONLY; play MUST NOT modify it or any other artifact produced by prepare
- C6: Output artifact path is `{stm_base}/{issue}/context/design/reference-algorithms.md` â€” sibling to tech.yaml in the existing context package
- C7: Agent is `tech-architect` (reuse); new skill is `draft-reference-algorithms` placed at `core/components/skills/draft-reference-algorithms/SKILL.md`
- C8: `draft-reference-algorithms` skill receives: `tech_yaml_path`, list of qualifying `interface_ids`, `stm_base`, `issue` â€” nothing else
- C9: Output artifact is a Markdown file structured by interface ID: one `## {interface_id}` section per qualifying interface, each containing: complexity classification, language-agnostic pseudocode block, invariants list, edge cases list
- C10: Play follows Structure B (direct-generation): pre-flight â†’ execution â†’ approval â†’ evidence
- C11: Play MUST NOT read from KB or LTM during execution; context is STM-only
- C12: tech-architect dispatched via ADR 016 JSON contract
- C13: Pre-flight verifies: stm_base resolves, issue resolves, tech.yaml exists and is not empty
- C14: Evidence & Close phase is gated by `evidence.record` from `.garura/core/config.yaml` (default `true`). When `true`: write evidence file, status file, invoke repo-orchestrator self-commit. When `false`: skip all three. User-facing summary is always presented regardless of flag value.
- C15: Compiled SKILL.md MUST include `## Task DAG` section per ADR 013
- C16: Skill output is DRAFT until tech-architect marks it LOCKED; approval step gates the lock
- C17: Play must not set or alter any `status` field in plan.yaml or milestone status files

**Failure Conditions:**
- F1: stm_base cannot be resolved from config.yaml â†’ Hard halt
- F2: tech.yaml not found â†’ Hard halt (prepare must run first)
- F3: tech.yaml is empty or unparseable â†’ Hard halt
- F4: No complexity indicators found AND no `algorithm_spec_required: true` flag â†’ Graceful exit (exit 0)
- F5: `draft-reference-algorithms` skill returns error after 2 recovery attempts â†’ Hard halt
- F6: `reference-algorithms.md` not written to expected path â†’ Hard halt
- F7: Prepare context package incomplete (missing plan.yaml, scenarios.yaml) â†’ Warn and continue

---

### Summary

The play introduces one new directory (`core/components/plays/algorithm/`) and one new skill (`core/components/skills/draft-reference-algorithms/`). It reuses `tech-architect` as the executing agent â€” no new agent requires the full P1-P11 audit cycle. The output artifact enters the existing context package convention without extending any locked schema. Zero modifications to prepare or implement.

The consumption pathway is explicit: `reference-algorithms.md` is placed in `context/design/` and serves as normative guidance for human implementers and code reviewers. It does NOT programmatically reach code-builder in the current implement play. The implement Step 1 tech-designer contract accepts exactly `plan_yaml_path`, `tech_yaml_path`, `architecture_context_path`, `milestone_id` â€” adding `reference_algorithms_path` is future work tracked as a risk item.

The SKILL.md for this play is compiled â€” **not hand-authored**. The source of truth is `core/components/plays/algorithm/reference/intent.yaml`. After authoring that file, run `/create-play --build algorithm` to compile the SKILL.md. Do not edit SKILL.md directly.

### Affected Files (New)

| File | Role | Change Needed |
|------|------|---------------|
| `core/components/plays/algorithm/reference/intent.yaml` | New source | Author intent definition â€” encode C1-C17, F1-F7, scenarios |
| `core/components/plays/algorithm/SKILL.md` | New compiled | Compiled by `/create-play --build algorithm` â€” do not edit manually |
| `core/components/skills/draft-reference-algorithms/SKILL.md` | New skill | Pseudocode generation skill for tech-architect to invoke |

### Affected Files (Read, No Modification)

| File | Role | Change Needed |
|------|------|---------------|
| `core/components/agents/tech-architect.md` | Agent identity for dispatch | None |
| `core/components/plays/create-play/SKILL.md` | Play compilation workflow | None |
| `core/components/plays/create-play/reference/workflows/direct-generation.yaml` | Structure B template | None |
| `core/components/skills/draft-lld/SKILL.md` | Canonical pattern reference for new skill | None |
| `core/components/plays/implement/SKILL.md` | Verify no pre-flight assertion closes context/design/ | None |
| `.garura/core/config.yaml` | stm_base path resolution + evidence.record flag | None |

### Technical Approach

**Strategy:** Author `core/components/plays/algorithm/reference/intent.yaml` (encoding all constraints and failure conditions), author the `draft-reference-algorithms` skill (modeled on draft-lld pattern), then compile the play via `/create-play --build algorithm`. The play uses Structure B (direct-generation). Pre-flight scans tech.yaml for 11 complexity indicator terms plus the explicit `algorithm_spec_required: true` flag; if none found, exits 0 gracefully without dispatching tech-architect. Evidence writing in the close phase is gated by `evidence.record` in config â€” the same pattern as `commit-code`.

**Alternatives Considered:**
- Extend prepare to produce reference algorithms â†’ Rejected: violates prepare's interface-level-only design; would couple all prepare runs to algorithm generation whether needed or not
- Embed pseudocode directly in tech.yaml â†’ Rejected: tech.yaml is a structured YAML contract, not a narrative document; mixing pseudocode prose into YAML fields destroys schema clarity
- Modify implement to call an algorithm sub-step â†’ Rejected: violates the constraint that implement must not be modified; also forces algorithm generation per milestone rather than per epic

**Risks:**

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|------|----------|-----------|
| `reference-algorithms.md` does not reach code-builder programmatically in current implement | HIGH | By design. Future work: add nullable `reference_algorithms_path` to tech-designer stm.input in implement's intent.yaml |
| Complexity scan false negatives for domain-specific vocabulary | MEDIUM | `algorithm_spec_required: true` flag is the escape hatch â€” authors set it explicitly |
| tech-architect cost on CRUD epics | LOW | Pre-flight graceful exit ensures agent never dispatched without qualifying interfaces |
| Artifact in context/design/ but not in prepare's C36 output list | LOW | Reference-algorithms.md is produced by a separate play; additive sibling, not part of prepare's closed list |
